title: Cyclobothra flava
intro: Cyclobothra flava, also known as the Yellow Cyclobothra, is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as arthritis and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclobothra flava include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cyclobothra flava is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is part of the subfamily Faboideae and is native to Australia. Within the subfamily Faboideae, Cyclobothra flava is placed in the tribe Podalyrieae. This species is also referred to by some sources as Podalyria flava. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Fabaceae family.
